description_long: This is a classic wooden chessboard set up for play, featuring a square board with alternating light and dark brown squares. The board is bordered by a slightly raised wooden frame, which is decorated with alphanumeric coordinates along the edges for easy reference. The chess pieces are traditional Staunton-style, with one side in a dark, almost black wood and the other in a light, natural wood finish. Each piece is finely carved, with distinct shapes for pawns, rooks, knights, bishops, queens, and kings. The board and pieces rest flat, suggesting a sturdy and stable construction suitable for outdoor or park use. The overall appearance is elegant and functional, with a tactile, polished wood surface.
description: A classic wooden chessboard with alternating light and dark squares, bordered by a wooden frame with alphanumeric markings and traditional Staunton-style chess pieces in light and dark wood.
description_view_0: The chessboard is viewed from the side of the dark pieces, showing the full lineup of black chessmen facing the light pieces across the board.
description_view_1: This view is from the side of the light pieces, highlighting the white chessmen in the foreground and the black pieces in the background.
description_view_2: The board is seen from the right side, displaying both sets of pieces in profile and emphasizing the alternating square pattern.
description_view_3: The chessboard is viewed from the side of the black pieces, with the black chessmen in the foreground and the white pieces across the board.
attribute_board_pattern: The playing surface features an 8x8 grid of perfectly square tiles, alternating between light and dark brown wood. The squares are arranged in a regular, checkerboard pattern, and the border is marked with alphanumeric coordinates for each row and column.
attribute_border_design: The border is a medium-width wooden frame, slightly raised above the playing surface, with a natural brown finish. It is decorated with white alphanumeric coordinates (A-H, 1-8) along each edge, aiding in move notation. The border is smooth and unornamented except for these markings.
